creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) Lovable signs multiyear deal with Google Cloud to up usage 5x, source says
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: Lovable and Google signed an expanded multiyear deal that involves a 5x expansion of Lovable's footprint on Google Cloud, and expanded access to Anthropic Claude.

creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) Alphabet’s record-breaking $85B raise for Google’s AI business is a helluva good signal
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: If Alphabet's record-breaking $85 billion stock sale signals investor appetite for AI-related offerings, we can see that investors are ready to chow.
contentSnippet: If Alphabet's record-breaking $85 billion stock sale signals investor appetite for AI-related offerings, we can see that investors are ready to chow.

title: (No AI) Amazon develops a warehouse robot workers can speak to
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/proteus.jpeg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

The design hasn’t changed much from the original Proteus, which was announced in 2022. | Image: Amazon

Amazon has announced a new version of its fully autonomous warehouse robot, [Proteus](https://www.theverge.com/2022/6/21/23177756/amazon-warehouse-robots-proteus-autonomous-cart-delivery), that will can interact using language instead of code. The expanded capabilities come as part of a growing pivot toward automation as the e-commerce giant replaces its human workers with robots.

Amazon says the AI-powered upgrade means its human employees can assign the robot tasks in the same way they'd communicate with colleagues. Previously, workers would need to use specialized software to direct the floor-level, tortoise-like systems, which are designed for heavy lifting and moving large carts throughout Amazon's warehouses. "You tell it what needs to …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/942884/amazon-next-generation-warehouse-robot-proteus)

Amazon says the AI-powered upgrade means its human employees can assign the robot tasks in the same way they'd communicate with colleagues. Previously, workers would need to use specialized software to direct the floor-level, tortoise-like systems, which are designed for heavy lifting and moving large carts throughout Amazon's warehouses. "You tell it what needs to …

title: (No AI) As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise
author: TC. Sottek
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/11503853/mdoying_180419_1777_0454_2.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

This week we've got tandem hands-ons with Google's new Gemini AI agent - Spark - from my colleagues [David Pierce](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/941388/gemini-spark-ai-agent-trip-planning) and [Jay Peters](https://www.theverge.com/tech/941138/google-gemini-spark-ai-agent-hands-on). Their takeaways are similar: It's so effective that it's scary. Spark knew that David's dog is named Frida and knew the first name of Jay's wife, even though neither of them explicitly provided this information to Google. But what's scary to _me_ is how all of this stuff seems geared toward a future of "productivity" that completely misses what needs to be fixed in our world.

"Productivity" is often pitched as a panacea for what befalls us in our personal lives, even going so far as to implicate our moral worthiness …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/942629/as-ai-gets-better-it-reveals-an-empty-promise)

But what's scary to me is how all of this stuff seems geared toward a future of "productivity" that completely misses what needs to be fixed in our world.
"Productivity" is often pitched as a panacea for what befalls us in our personal lives, even going so far as to implicate our moral worthiness …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Jeff Bezos Is Funding a Wild Hunt for the Brain’s ‘Core Algorithm’

creator: Todd Feathers
title: (No AI) Alpha School’s Ritzy New York City Campus Costs $65,000 a Year—but Isn’t Actually a School
dc:creator: Todd Feathers
content: A homeschooling center in Manhattan is part of the company’s nationwide expansion. Internal documents reveal its strategy: “Opening date > safety.”
contentSnippet: A homeschooling center in Manhattan is part of the company’s nationwide expansion. Internal documents reveal its strategy: “Opening date > safety.”
